title: Partial Radio Intercept: Possible Moscow Broadcast
description: In the radio room log, a single entry at 19:12: 'Emergency frequency, carrier wave detected, duration 40 seconds, encoded text fragments, classification: atmospheric interference.' Below it, in different handwriting — Volkov's — a single line added in pencil: 'Carrier wave modulation consistent with Moscow VLF transmitter. Not confirmed. Not reported.' The transmission was heard. It was not logged as a signal. If it was Moscow, it may have been the stand-down order. It will never be confirmed with the equipment aboard.
